message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker metadata for 1mfw3 subtasks

Refresh .beads/issues.jsonl with updated dependency links and status
for the FrankenTUI command palette integration subtasks (1mfw3.1.x)
and related responsive layout work. Also bump the last-touched marker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","title":"Replace custom palette overlay rendering with ftui command_palette widget","description":"Subtask .1.3

Objective:
Swap manual overlay rendering path to use FrankenTUI command_palette rendering primitives while preserving cass visual language.

Deliverables:
- Overlay rendering integration in app view path.
- Selection/highlight/scroll behavior delegated to widget logic.
- Styling alignment with cass theme system.

Rationale:
Using battle-tested widget behavior reduces bespoke rendering bugs and unlocks advanced palette capabilities.

Done when:
The palette opens/renders/navigates through widget-backed code rather than manual row formatting.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:06.050805507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.695380531Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:06.050805507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.695325368Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","title":"Add match-type filters and explicit filter controls in palette","description":"Subtask .1.4

Objective:
Expose match-mode filtering controls (all/exact/prefix/word-start/substring/fuzzy) in cass palette UX.

Deliverables:
- Keyboard controls for filter mode cycle and direct mode selection.
- Visual indicator of active filter mode.
- Graceful behavior when filters produce zero results.

Rationale:
Filter controls make ranking behavior transparent and increase precision for expert users.

Done when:
Users can intentionally select match modes and observe deterministic result set changes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.358901842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.981454650Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.358901842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:54.981368790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","title":"Expose palette ranking evidence ledger in UI","description":"Subtask .1.5

Objective:
Render a compact evidence ledger for selected palette results so users can inspect why a command ranked where it did.

Deliverables:
- Evidence summary panel for selected result.
- Human-readable components (match type, score contributors, confidence hints).
- Empty-state messaging when evidence is unavailable.

Rationale:
Explainability builds operator trust and reduces perceived randomness in fuzzy search.

Done when:
Palette ranking can be inspected in-product without external logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.450954880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.275942695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.450954880Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.275874709Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","title":"Palette migration regression tests and deterministic behavior lock-in","description":"Objective:
Establish comprehensive tests for palette migration to guarantee behavior parity and deterministic operation.

Deliverables:
- Tests for open/close, navigation, query filtering, execution dispatch.
- Tests for match filter mode behavior and zero-result handling.
- Tests for evidence/latency surfaces under nominal and empty data.

Rationale:
Palette is a high-frequency operator surface; regressions here have outsized usability cost.

Done when:
Test coverage guards the migrated palette behavior against future regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.693580233Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:57.406086560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.383079912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.693489133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","title":"Palette migration regression tests and deterministic behavior lock-in","description":"Objective:
Build comprehensive automated test coverage for palette migration with both unit-level guarantees and end-to-end behavior validation.

Deliverables:
- Extensive unit tests for open/close, navigation, filtering, execution dispatch, and deterministic ordering behavior.
- Dedicated e2e script(s) exercising palette lifecycle flows (open -> type -> filter mode -> navigate -> execute -> close).
- Structured detailed logging from e2e runs (JSONL or equivalent) including query, match_count, selected_action, latency, and assertion outcomes.
- Human-readable test summary artifact linking failed assertions to exact scenario steps.

Rationale:
Palette is a high-frequency operator surface; reliability and debuggability must be proven at both logic and interaction layers.

Done when:
Unit tests and e2e scripts both pass consistently, with rich logs sufficient for fast post-failure diagnosis.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:06:51.492830905Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:22.959385335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:57.406086560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.383079912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:58.693489133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","title":"Add palette query latency instrumentation and micro-bench toggle","description":"Objective:
Add low-overhead latency instrumentation for palette query updates and an opt-in micro-bench mode to detect performance regressions.

Deliverables:
- Last-query latency capture.
- Optional benchmark mode with throughput signal (queries/sec).
- Visual budget indicator for query responsiveness.

Rationale:
Making latency visible helps prevent UX regressions as command catalog/features grow.

Done when:
Operators can quickly verify palette responsiveness from within the TUI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597488555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597408966Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","title":"Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI","description":"Workstream .2: Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI.

Background:
cass currently runs on ftui runtime with budget + mouse enabled, but does not fully wire advanced ProgramConfig knobs for resize evidence and regime-aware coalescing. FrankenTUI includes a resize coalescer with BOCPD support and decision logs.

Goals:
1) Wire ProgramConfig with explicit resize coalescer settings (logging + BOCPD).
2) Capture/summarize resize decisions in cass-facing model state.
3) Surface resize behavior in inspector so users can diagnose resize storms.
4) Keep behavior deterministic and robust across fullscreen/inline/macro paths.

In scope:
- run_tui_ftui config unification and upgrade.
- Evidence plumbing and summary model.
- Inspector presentation of resize regime/action/evidence.
- Test coverage for resize storms and fallback behavior.

Out of scope:
- Full explainability cockpit UX (tracked in .3).

Validation expectations:
- No regressions in macro record/playback paths.
- Resize behavior remains stable under rapid terminal changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34.870097395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: treat runtime config parity as non-negotiable. Any knob added for standard mode must be reviewed for record/playback + inline/fullscreen paths. Evidence wiring should fail soft (graceful empty state) rather than fail closed (broken UI path).","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","title":"Unify ProgramConfig construction across standard, record, and playback paths","description":"Objective:
Refactor run_tui_ftui so all execution modes share one explicit ProgramConfig construction path.

Deliverables:
- Common config builder with mode-specific deltas only where required.
- Consistent runtime knobs across standard, macro recording, and playback paths.
- Reduced configuration drift risk.

Why this matters:
Without a unified builder, advanced runtime options are easy to wire in one path and forget in others, creating invisible behavioral inconsistency.

Done when:
All launch paths consume the same config baseline and differences are intentional and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","title":"Enable BOCPD + logging CoalescerConfig profile for cass runtime","description":"Objective:
Select and wire an explicit resize coalescer profile with BOCPD enabled and decision logging turned on.

Deliverables:
- CoalescerConfig settings for steady/burst/deadline behavior.
- BOCPD enabled with sane defaults for cass interaction profile.
- Logging configuration suitable for UI-facing evidence summaries.

Why this matters:
Regime-aware resize behavior is core to responsiveness under resize storms; logging is required for explainability and debugging.

Done when:
Runtime is using explicit BOCPD/logging settings in all relevant launch modes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.009769383Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.009687150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","title":"Wire resize decision evidence capture path into cass model layer","description":"Objective:
Capture resize decision/evidence signals emitted by runtime and make them available to cass model state.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","title":"Upgrade inspector to explainability cockpit (diff/resize/budget timeline)","description":"Workstream .3: Upgrade inspector to explainability cockpit (diff/resize/budget timeline).

Background:
Current inspector has useful timing/layout/hit-region tabs but limited causal explanation. FrankenTUI showcase demonstrates explainability cockpit patterns that unify diff strategy, resize regime, and budget/degradation decisions.

Goals:
1) Expand inspector into a practical explainability cockpit.
2) Provide compact timeline context for adaptive runtime decisions.
3) Make performance/debug investigations possible without external log spelunking.

In scope:
- New/expanded inspector tab architecture.
- Panels for diff strategy summary, resize summary, budget/degradation summary.
- Timeline feed correlating major decision events.
- Responsive overlay behavior for constrained terminals.

Out of scope:
- Offline analytics dashboards outside TUI runtime loop.

Validation expectations:
- Cockpit renders safely across degradation levels and terminal sizes.
- Added data never crashes when evidence is absent/partial.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.853555598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:35.627601254Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.853555598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:02.157691206Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Design note: cockpit UX should optimize for diagnosis latency. Prefer compact, high-signal fields and causal timeline snippets over verbose text dumps. The best cockpit answers: what changed, why it changed, and what the system is doing now.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.1","title":"Define explainability cockpit information architecture and data contract","description":"Objective:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","title":"P2.2 Indexer multi-root orchestration","description":"# Indexer Multi-Root Orchestration

## Context
The indexer needs to build a list of scan roots and orchestrate scanning across them, injecting provenance appropriately.

## Location
src/indexer/mod.rs

## Current Flow
1. Build single ScanContext with data_dir
2. For each connector, call scan()
3. Persist results

## New Flow
1. Build list of scan roots:
   - Local roots (from watch_roots() or equivalent)
   - Remote mirror roots (from sources table)
2. Group roots by connector affinity
3. For each connector:
   - For each relevant root:
     - Create ScanContext with that root
     - Call scan()
     - Inject provenance into results
     - Persist with source_id

## Implementation

### Build Scan Roots List
\`\`\`rust
fn build_scan_roots(storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Vec<ScanRoot> {
    let mut roots = Vec::new();
    
    // Add local roots (existing logic from watch_roots())
    for local_root in local_default_roots() {
        roots.push(ScanRoot {
            path: local_root,
            origin: Origin::local(),
            platform: Some(Platform::current()),
            workspace_rewrites: vec![],
        });
    }
    
    // Add remote mirror roots
    for source in storage.list_sources()? {
        if source.kind == SourceKind::Ssh {
            let mirror_root = data_dir.join("remotes").join(&source.id).join("mirror");
            if mirror_root.exists() {
                roots.push(ScanRoot {
                    path: mirror_root,
                    origin: Origin {
                        source_id: source.id.clone(),
                        kind: source.kind,
                        host: source.host_label.clone(),
                    },
                    platform: source.platform,
                    workspace_rewrites: parse_rewrites(&source.config),
                });
            }
        }
    }
    
    roots
}
\`\`\`

### Connector Affinity
Not all roots apply to all connectors. For example:
- ClaudeCodeConnector cares about ~/.claude or remotes/*/mirror/**/.claude
- AiderConnector cares about .aider.chat.history.md anywhere

For now: let connectors handle root relevance via their detect() logic.

### Provenance Injection
After connector.scan() returns:
\`\`\`rust
for conv in conversations.iter_mut() {
    conv.metadata["cass"] = json!({
        "origin": {
            "source_id": scan_root.origin.source_id,
            "kind": scan_root.origin.kind.as_str(),
            "host": scan_root.origin.host,
        }
    });
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- P2.1 (ScanContext extended)
- P1.3 (storage accepts source_id)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Indexer builds list of scan roots
- [ ] Local roots work as before
- [ ] Provenance injected into metadata
- [ ] Results persisted with correct source_id
- [ ] Tests verify multi-root behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:57:31.805169Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","close_reason":"Implemented multi-root orchestration with build_scan_roots(), inject_provenance(), and provenance extraction in persist::map_to_internal().
